articles.headline: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft for AI Gadgets
articles.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit alleging OpenAI stole over 400 Apple engineers' trade secrets to develop unrevealed AI gadgets, creating the single largest legal risk ahead of OpenAI's confidential $730 billion IPO filing in September[4][6]. This litigation could delay or derail OpenAI's public debut, impacting investor confidence and AI infrastructure investment plans for enterprise partners.

articles.headline: TSMC to Invest $100 Billion More in U.S. After AI-Fueled Earnings Surge
articles.summary: TSMC posted its fifth consecutive quarter of record earnings driven by global AI infrastructure demand and announced a further $100 billion investment in U.S. manufacturing[4]. This expansion signals sustained chip supply growth but also underscores the $1 trillion+ spending boom in AI infrastructure that may face sustainability concerns if productivity gains lag[1].

articles.headline: Chinese Startup Moonshot Unveils World's Largest Open AI Model, Closing Gap on U.S. Rivals
articles.summary: Moonshot launched Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion-parameter open-weight model approaching Anthropic's frontier performance, intensifying global competition in AI capabilities[1]. Enterprises evaluating AI vendors may face new cost and compliance considerations as open models from non-U.S. firms become more competitive, potentially reshaping cloud and inference pricing dynamics.
